title: Modes, Padding, Malleability, and Legacy Decryption · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Ctr Counter Reuse
summary: A database encrypts records with ECB, a migration endpoint exposes CBC padding failures, and a CTR counter can repeat after restoring a snapshot. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ctr counter reuse increment in mode failure laboratory and migration adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Trace CTR keystream generation and prove that key-counter reuse destroys confidentiality and enables malleation.
Trace CBC chaining, IV requirements, padding validation, malleability, and oracle behavior as a legacy risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Ctr Counter Reuse · Theory Crypto Go Ctr Counter Reuse
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 5678 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ECB and unauthenticated modes while containing CBC or CTR to uniform decrypt-only migration; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace CTR keystream generation and prove that key-counter reuse destroys confidentiality and enables malleation.
